automotive-ev-battery.title: Samsung SDI reportedly to supply battery cells to Volkswagen
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen is expanding its battery supply chain by bringing South Korean manufacturer Samsung SDI on board as an additional supplier for its standardized Unified Cell. This diversification of suppliers supplements existing production from VW subsidiary PowerCo in Salzgitter and affiliate Gotion in China.

automotive-ev-battery.title: UK EV market share reaches 27.3% in strongest May since 2019
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Battery-electric vehicle registrations in the UK surged 34.2% year-over-year in May 2026, achieving a 27.3% market share—the highest monthly BEV share recorded so far in 2026. The milestone reflects continued acceleration in EV adoption across the UK market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Moment Energy opens new second-life facility in Vancouver
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Canadian battery recycling firm Moment Energy is commissioning a new facility in Vancouver dedicated to repurposing used electric vehicle batteries for stationary energy storage systems. The plant is expected to reach annual capacity of 1 GWh, supporting the circular economy for EV batteries.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Sany delivers long-haul electric trucks in Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese truck manufacturer Sany has begun commercial operations of its e263 electric semi-truck in Europe, with initial customer vehicles now operating in Germany. The company plans to unveil additional models and provide a 2027 preview at the IAA Transportation event.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Rivian Adventure Network announces fast charging milestone
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Rivian has announced that its proprietary fast charging network now exceeds 1,000 DC fast charging ports across the United States. The expansion included opening four new sites with 44 total charging stalls in the past month alone.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Ireland announces €10m in funding for EV scrappage scheme
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ireland has launched a government-funded scrappage initiative offering consumers up to €5,000 in additional incentives to trade in internal combustion engine vehicles registered in 2013 or earlier for new battery electric vehicles. The €10 million program aims to accelerate the transition from aging ICE vehicles to EVs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Xpeng to produce fourth model at Magna Steyr in Graz
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer Xpeng is expanding its production partnership with contract manufacturer Magna Steyr in Austria, adding a fourth model to the facility's existing lineup of the G6, G9, and P7+. The identity of the additional vehicle remains unconfirmed pending official announcement.

automotive-ev-battery.title: KIA UK expands PV5 lineup with Cargo and Passenger additions
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia UK has announced new entry-level Cargo and seven-seat Passenger variants of its PV5 electric vehicle for the 2027 model year, along with additional refinements to existing models. The expanded lineup broadens the company's commercial and passenger EV offerings.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mercedes F1 team deploys eActros 600 across European season
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team will operate a battery-electric Mercedes-Benz eActros 600 truck throughout all nine European races of the season, making it the first F1 team to employ an electric truck across an entire European race campaign. The deployment demonstrates electric vehicle capability in high-performance logistics operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: First drive in the MG4 EV Urban: Not exciting – but surprisingly good
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MG Motor's new MG4 EV Urban prioritizes practical everyday utility and straightforward electric mobility over flashy design or advanced technology features. A test drive evaluation across urban traffic, country roads, and motorway conditions assesses whether the pragmatic approach delivers real-world value.

finance-markets.title: Goldman Sachs expects SpaceX's AI revenue to increase 100-fold by 2030
finance-markets.summary: Goldman Sachs projects SpaceX's artificial intelligence revenue will grow 100-fold by 2030, providing a key valuation pillar for the company's anticipated initial public offering pitched at $1.78 trillion. The bank's optimistic forecast underscores Wall Street's confidence in the intersection of SpaceX's satellite infrastructure and emerging AI applications.

finance-markets.title: Bond Traders Expecting Fed Hike Look Ahead to Jobs Data Risk
finance-markets.summary: Treasury bond traders have positioned for Federal Reserve rate increases over the next 12 months, but face significant downside risk if May employment data disappoint. The market's current positioning could result in substantial losses if labor market conditions weaken from expectations.

finance-markets.title: US Futures, Korean Stocks Drop as AI Mania Fades: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Asian equities and US equity-index futures declined as enthusiasm for artificial intelligence investments cooled following a period of record market highs driven by AI-related optimism. The market pullback signals potential consolidation after AI stocks' recent exceptional performance.

finance-markets.title: Tech groups find a new way to monetise AI hype: selling volatility
finance-markets.summary: Technology companies are issuing elevated levels of convertible bonds as a strategy to capitalize on AI enthusiasm while managing market volatility. The trend reflects companies' efforts to raise capital amid elevated market uncertainty and investor sentiment swings.

finance-markets.title: Fuel Shocks, Rising Prices Threaten India's Consumption Growth
finance-markets.summary: Global energy crises and rising commodity prices are threatening to slow consumption among India's middle class, undermining the country's fragile consumption recovery amid broader currency and market pressures. The energy-driven inflation presents a significant headwind to India's economic growth trajectory.

finance-markets.title: SpaceX IPO shows Musk's genius is in mythmaking
finance-markets.summary: SpaceX's IPO valuation rests on ambitious projections that may lack clear revenue justification, highlighting Elon Musk's capacity for narrative building in financial markets. The company's ability to support its gargantuan valuation through concrete revenue streams remains uncertain.

finance-markets.title: Korean Stocks Slide as Cracks Emerge in World-Beating Rally
finance-markets.summary: South Korea's $4.9 trillion stock market is showing signs of strain and potential weakness following an exceptional rally, with breadth concerns emerging across the index. The market's deteriorating momentum suggests possible consolidation or pullback after strong recent performance.

finance-markets.title: Fuji Media's Real Estate Unit Attracts $6.3 Billion Bids
finance-markets.summary: Fuji Media Holdings' real estate subsidiary has attracted stronger-than-expected bidding interest from major financial institutions including KKR, Blackstone, and Goldman Sachs, potentially valued around $6.3 billion. The robust auction demand reflects continued investor appetite for premium real estate assets in Japan.

finance-markets.title: Trump's pledge to unleash a 'golden age' of US manufacturing sputters
finance-markets.summary: Spending on new US factory construction has declined while industry executives report uneven economic recovery, undermining the administration's manufacturing revitalization agenda. The slowdown in capital expenditure suggests skepticism among corporate leaders regarding the sustainability of manufacturing growth initiatives.

finance-markets.title: Philippine Inflation Eases in May Amid Fuel Price Rollbacks
finance-markets.summary: Philippine inflation decelerated in May as fuel prices declined following optimism around US-Iran ceasefire talks, reducing immediate pressure for monetary policy tightening. The moderation in price growth provides the central bank with greater flexibility in near-term rate decisions.

world-news.title: Zelensky proposes face-to-face talks in open letter to Putin
world-news.summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky has called for direct engagement with Russian President Vladimir Putin as the only viable path to ending the ongoing war. The proposal comes as the United States shifts diplomatic focus toward Iran-related matters.

world-news.title: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
world-news.summary: Hezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon announced by the United States following recent diplomatic negotiations. The rejection threatens to prolong regional tensions despite the framework's acceptance by the Israeli and Lebanese governments.

world-news.title: Trump hits back at 'unpatriotic' vote after House rebukes him over Iran
world-news.summary: The US House of Representatives passed a measure seeking to halt further military action against Iran, in a largely symbolic vote that drew criticism from the Trump administration. The vote reflects ongoing congressional division over military engagement in the Middle East.

world-news.title: Democrat fails to block US measure to deepen Israel military cooperation
world-news.summary: Congressman Ro Khanna's effort to revoke legislation deepening US-Israel military cooperation fell short in a congressional vote. The measure passed despite arguments that increased integration primarily benefits Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's agenda.

world-news.title: Why Mogadishu clashes are deepening Somalia's political crisis again
world-news.summary: Tensions over Somali elections have escalated into violent clashes in Mogadishu, exacerbating the nation's ongoing political instability. The violence has created widespread fear and disruption among residents amid deepening political divisions.

world-news.title: Kenyan president defends US Ebola facility amid deadly protests
world-news.summary: Kenyan President William Ruto defended the decision to allow the United States to construct an Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya, characterizing it as strategically important. The facility has sparked deadly public protests and raised concerns about sovereignty and public safety.

world-news.title: Why is the US so invested in Colombia's election?
world-news.summary: The Trump administration has demonstrated significant interest in Colombia's presidential election outcomes, reflecting broader US strategic concerns in the Latin American region. American officials' involvement signals the geopolitical importance Washington places on Colombian political developments.

world-news.title: US defence secretary compares Bolivia protests to government 'overthrow'
world-news.summary: The Trump administration's defence secretary characterized recent Bolivian protests as resembling a government 'overthrow' in remarks supporting President Rodrigo Paz following his contested electoral victory. The statement reflects US backing for Paz amid opposition from the left-wing MAS party.

world-news.title: Democrats force vote on Trump's $1.8bn settlement fund in 'vote-a-rama'
world-news.summary: Congressional Democrats forced a vote on the Trump administration's $1.8 billion settlement fund during a parliamentary procedure known as 'vote-a-rama' while Republicans pursued $70 billion in immigration-enforcement funding. The legislative maneuver highlighted partisan divisions over immigration policy and government spending.

world-news.title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
world-news.summary: United States authorities are implementing biological control measures using sterile flies and detection dogs to combat a flesh-eating screwworm outbreak in the southern US, marking the insect's first documented presence in America since 1966. The pest poses a significant agricultural and public health threat requiring international coordination.

tech-ai.title: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: 'We can only support so much'
tech-ai.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., the world's largest chipmaker, is struggling to meet surging demand from American customers despite expanding U.S. factory capacity. CEO C.C. Wei acknowledged that customer demand for AI chips is exceptionally high and stated the company is working to ensure TSMC does not become a bottleneck in the AI supply chain.

tech-ai.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic's Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI's returns
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic has demonstrated explosive growth with annualized revenue crossing $47 billion in May, a substantial increase from approximately $9 billion at the end of 2025, as the AI startup prepares for an initial public offering. The company's trajectory now faces a critical test to sustain its rapid expansion and validate its business model.

tech-ai.title: Helion, the Sam Altman-backed fusion startup, raises $465M to build a power plant for Microsoft
tech-ai.summary: Helion Energy, backed by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, has secured $465 million in funding to accelerate construction of a fusion power plant for Microsoft scheduled for completion by 2028. The investment underscores the critical role fusion energy is expected to play in powering the massive computational demands of AI infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
tech-ai.summary: Poke has become the first AI agent approved for Apple's Messages for Business platform, enabling users to interact with artificial intelligence through simple text messages. The approval marks a significant milestone in integrating AI agents into Apple's enterprise communications ecosystem.

tech-ai.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
tech-ai.summary: Meta has adopted an unconventional cost-reduction strategy by constructing temporary data centers in tents to manage its substantial infrastructure expenses. The approach reflects the company's efforts to rapidly scale its computational capacity amid massive investments in AI infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: Airbnb's Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
tech-ai.summary: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky has announced plans to establish an artificial intelligence laboratory as the company explores deeper integration of AI capabilities into its platform. Chesky previously indicated that existing large language model partnerships were not yet mature enough to meet Airbnb's requirements.

tech-ai.title: Meta's Oversight Board says account bans lack due process, transparency
tech-ai.summary: Meta's independent Oversight Board has raised concerns about the lack of due process and transparency in the company's account suspension decisions. The board is urging Meta to provide clearer information regarding policy violations and improve transparency regarding the company's use of AI in moderation determinations.

tech-ai.title: Windows is back on the Microsoft menu
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella prioritized Windows during the company's Build keynote, showcasing the new Surface RTX Spark Dev Kit designed for AI development. The emphasis on Windows for AI computing comes as Nvidia officially returns to the Windows on Arm platform with its new RTX Spark chips.

tech-ai.title: Google is letting social media stars customize their search result page
tech-ai.summary: Google has introduced a new feature enabling prominent creators and publishers in the United States to claim dedicated profiles in Search that showcase their videos, articles, and other online profiles. The feature is currently limited to individuals with at least 100,000 YouTube subscribers, 100,000 followers on Instagram or X, or 300,000 TikTok followers.

tech-ai.title: Filtr is a new privacy tool that blocks ads in almost every iPhone and Mac app
tech-ai.summary: Filtr, an ad blocking application for iPhones, iPads, and Macs, has introduced a new feature that blocks advertisements from loading within apps, including web browsers, leveraging recent capabilities in Apple's latest operating system software. The tool enhances user privacy by preventing ads from displaying across Apple's ecosystem.

science-space.title: Scientists finally crack an 'undruggable' pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
science-space.summary: Researchers have successfully developed daraxonrasib, a drug that targets the KRAS mutation responsible for most pancreatic tumors, overcoming a challenge long considered insurmountable. Clinical trials demonstrate the treatment nearly doubled survival rates for patients with advanced pancreatic cancer and reduced mortality risk by 60%, representing a major breakthrough in one of the most lethal cancers.

science-space.title: Meteorite found in Sahara desert may be 1st evidence of lost solar system world
science-space.summary: A rare meteorite recovered from the Sahara Desert contains evidence of a previously unknown moon-sized celestial body that existed only a few million years after the solar system's formation. This discovery provides unprecedented insight into the early solar system's composition and planetary architecture.

science-space.title: Scientists discover vast hidden structure beneath Antarctica's ice
science-space.summary: Researchers have identified a massive fan-shaped network of subglacial basins beneath East Antarctica, revealing that several previously distinct geological features are interconnected as one enormous structure. The discovery enhances understanding of Antarctica's tectonic history and may provide critical insights for modeling ice sheet behavior and predicting future changes.

science-space.title: Most exoplanets might be 'soot factories,' scientists say: 'Like you have a natural diesel engine'
science-space.summary: A chemical engineer's analysis of atmospheric spectra from mini-Neptune exoplanets has revealed that their hazy atmospheres contain soot-like compounds similar to those produced by combustion engines. This finding suggests that atmospheric chemistry on distant worlds may be far more complex and dynamic than previously understood.

science-space.title: Glittering star cluster image reveals missing patch of stars: 'We were not looking for the gap, but we found it'
science-space.summary: The Euclid space telescope has detected a subtle gap in the brightness distribution of red dwarf stars within the ancient globular cluster NGC 6397. This unexpected discovery offers new opportunities to understand the dynamics and evolution of these dense stellar systems.

science-space.title: NASA-Funded Study Shows Wildfire Smoke's Hidden Ozone Toll
science-space.summary: A new NASA-funded study documents how wildfire smoke has significantly increased ground-level ozone pollution across the contiguous United States over the past decade, creating hazardous air quality in regions far removed from active fire zones. The research underscores the far-reaching environmental impacts of wildfires beyond their immediate burn areas.

science-space.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is not an alien spacecraft: SETI hunt for 'technosignatures' comes up empty
science-space.summary: Scientists conducting a Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence (SETI) analysis of the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS found no radio signals or technological signatures, confirming its natural origin. The negative result adds to growing evidence that the object is a conventional comet rather than an artificial construct.

science-space.title: Goethe never knew this 40-million-year-old ant was hidden in his collection
science-space.summary: Advanced 3D imaging analysis of amber specimens from Johann Wolfgang von Goethe's personal collection has revealed three previously unknown fossil insects, including an exceptionally well-preserved extinct ant species. The detailed preservation provides unprecedented biological insights into the ant's morphology and suggests it likely constructed large arboreal nests.

science-space.title: August 2026 lunar eclipse: Everything you need to know about the 96% 'blood moon'
science-space.summary: A deep partial lunar eclipse on August 27-28, 2026, will achieve 96% totality and be visible throughout North America, making it the most significant lunar eclipse visible from the continent until New Year's Eve 2028. The event will offer rare observational opportunities for eclipse enthusiasts and scientists alike.

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4908-4912: Goodbye Campo Marte, It's Been Fun!
science-space.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully completed its 47th drilling operation on Mars at the Campo Marte site, conducting detailed mineralogical analysis during the extended drilling period. The rover's continued productive operations demonstrate the ongoing scientific value of the long-duration Mars exploration mission.

health-biotech.title: HHS confirms Americans with high-risk Ebola exposures will have access to experimental therapy
health-biotech.summary: The Department of Health and Human Services has confirmed that Americans exposed to Ebola during the current Central Africa outbreak will have access to an experimental antibody treatment. This marks an important public health response to a high-risk infectious disease threat.

health-biotech.title: Otsuka kidney drug slowed loss of function, but less than expected, in late-stage trial
health-biotech.summary: Otsuka's Voyxact demonstrated efficacy in slowing kidney function decline in patients with chronic autoimmune kidney disease, though the clinical benefit fell short of initial expectations in late-stage testing. The results carry significant implications for treatment options in IgAN and similar conditions.

health-biotech.title: Supreme Court backs generic drugmaker in 'skinny labeling' case
health-biotech.summary: The Supreme Court unanimously ruled in favor of Hikma Pharmaceuticals in a patent dispute with Amarin over so-called skinny labeling, a decision with far-reaching implications for the generic drug industry and future patent litigation. The ruling establishes important legal precedent for how generic manufacturers can label and market products.

health-biotech.title: Mammalian hair, roof leaks: Novo Nordisk's site in Indiana under FDA scrutiny once again
health-biotech.summary: Novo Nordisk's manufacturing facility in Bloomington, Indiana continues to face contamination challenges, with an FDA re-inspection in April identifying mammalian hair in finished products and water leaks that threaten product sterility. The recurring issues raise serious concerns about manufacturing quality control at this critical production site.

health-biotech.title: For pancreatic cancer patients, an exciting drug can feel out of reach
health-biotech.summary: Despite excitement surrounding a newly approved pancreatic cancer treatment, patients face significant concerns about access, availability, and whether supply will meet demand. The disparity between therapeutic promise and practical patient access represents a critical challenge in oncology care.

health-biotech.title: Drug companies, patient groups urge FDA to pause commissioner's voucher program
health-biotech.summary: Pharmaceutical companies and patient advocacy groups have called for a pause to the FDA commissioner's voucher program, citing concerns that the White House has used it to reward companies aligned with its political objectives. The controversy raises questions about the politicization of FDA incentive mechanisms.

health-biotech.title: Lilly, Boehringer retrench in Germany on proposed health spending cuts
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly and Boehringer Ingelheim have announced significant reductions in their German investments in response to sweeping health insurance reforms proposed by the German government. The manufacturers' pullback reflects broader industry concerns about the viability of the proposed health system overhaul.

health-biotech.title: Acting FDA commissioner pledges to prevent political interference in rare disease meeting
health-biotech.summary: Acting FDA Commissioner Kyle Diamantas met with executives from 15 rare disease nonprofits and explicitly committed to preventing political interference in the agency's regulatory processes. The statement signals a potential shift in FDA leadership direction regarding independence from political pressure.

health-biotech.title: Hikma to funnel $267M to Ohio; Cingulate gets CRL for ADHD drug
health-biotech.summary: Generic drugmaker Hikma announced a $267 million investment in Ohio facilities, while Cingulate received a Complete Response Letter from the FDA regarding its ADHD drug candidate. The mixed news reflects ongoing capital deployment in manufacturing alongside regulatory setbacks in novel therapeutics.

health-biotech.title: AI titans push Congress for DNA safeguards
health-biotech.summary: Major AI companies are advocating to Congress for regulatory safeguards around DNA synthesis and genetic information, reflecting growing concerns about biosecurity in an increasingly AI-driven biotechnology landscape. The push underscores emerging regulatory gaps in synthetic biology and artificial intelligence convergence.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A cybercrime group launched a data extortion attack against Canvas, a widely-used education technology platform, disrupting classes across U.S. school districts and universities. The attackers defaced the login page with ransom demands threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts, including those of the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force, were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian content after attackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. Instructions for the attack were circulated on Telegram, demonstrating a vulnerability in the automated password recovery process.

cybersecurity.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
cybersecurity.summary: A critical vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action allowed attackers to take over public repositories with a single GitHub issue, potentially enabling malicious code injection. The flaw posed a significant supply chain risk, as compromised code could be pushed downstream to dependent projects.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
cybersecurity.summary: Unknown attackers maintained access to a senior stock exchange executive's Outlook mailbox for at least five months, systematically copying inbox contents and routing them through cloud services to evade detection. The sophisticated operation indicates espionage objectives rather than financial gain.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
cybersecurity.summary: Cisco released a patch for a critical server-side request forgery vulnerability in Unified Communications Manager that allows unauthenticated network attackers to write files and escalate privileges to root. Public proof-of-concept exploit code has already been released, significantly reducing the window for organizations to apply the patch.

cybersecurity.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
cybersecurity.summary: A large-scale operation uses convincing counterfeit websites impersonating popular open-source and freeware projects to distribute malware through traffic distribution systems. The malware families delivered include Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and SessionGate, with fraudulent sites ranking highly in Google search results.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle released record-breaking volumes of security patches in May 2026, with AI systems proving effective at identifying vulnerabilities in human-written code. The rapid patch releases highlight the growing sophistication of AI-assisted vulnerability discovery.

cybersecurity.title: FlutterShell Backdoor Spreads to macOS via Malicious Google and YouTube Ads
cybersecurity.summary: A malvertising campaign called Operation FlutterBridge uses ads on Google and YouTube to distribute a new macOS backdoor named FlutterShell. The campaign represents an escalation of previous attack activity, demonstrating the effectiveness of ad-based malware distribution on major platforms.

cybersecurity.title: China-Linked TA4922 Expands Phishing Attacks to U.K., Germany, Italy, and South Africa
cybersecurity.summary: The China-linked cybercrime group TA4922 has significantly expanded its targeting scope to European and African organizations, utilizing a rapidly evolving malware arsenal including ValleyRAT and Atlas RAT variants. The group demonstrates sustained operational tempo and growing geographic ambitions.

cybersecurity.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice announced results from a coordinated disruption operation against Southeast Asia-based cyber fraud networks targeting Americans, successfully freezing $3.8 million in assets. The coordinated effort involved takedowns of millions of fraudulent social media, email, and internet access accounts used in transnational schemes.

startups-vc.title: Anthropic Files Confidentially For IPO
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic, a leading generative AI company, has submitted a confidential IPO filing, escalating the race among AI behemoths to reach the public market first. This move marks a significant milestone in the maturation of the AI startup ecosystem and signals investor confidence in the sector's long-term viability.

startups-vc.title: Helion, the Sam Altman-backed fusion startup, raises $465M to build a power plant for Microsoft
startups-vc.summary: Helion Energy has secured $465 million in funding to accelerate development of a power plant for Microsoft, targeting completion by 2028. The substantial capital raise underscores growing investor interest in fusion energy as a solution for powering data centers and AI infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: Quick-commerce FirstClub doubles valuation to $255M in 9 months
startups-vc.summary: FirstClub, a Bengaluru-based quick-commerce startup, has doubled its valuation to $255 million within nine months of launch and achieved over 1 million orders with a $50 million annualized GMV run rate. The rapid scaling demonstrates the continued momentum in India's competitive quick-commerce sector.

startups-vc.title: Exclusive: Scotch Raises $20M Series A To Disrupt Legacy Liquor Retail Tech With AI
startups-vc.summary: Scotch, a Denver-based AI-native operating system designed for liquor store owners, has raised $20 million in Series A funding to modernize legacy retail technology. The funding reflects growing investor appetite for AI-powered enterprise software solutions targeting fragmented vertical markets.

startups-vc.title: 20VC x SaaStr is Back!! Tokens Over Humans, the End of the SaaSpocalypse, and the Trillion-Dollar Land Grab
startups-vc.summary: The latest 20VC roundtable discussion highlights major trends reshaping B2B and AI investing, including Anthropic's confidential IPO filing following a $65 billion round and Cognition's $26 billion valuation. The conversation captures the current market dynamics as elite investors assess opportunities in the rapidly evolving AI-driven SaaS landscape.

startups-vc.title: They Saw Women Shut Out Of VC, So A PayPal Veteran And Former Navy Officer Built An Alternative
startups-vc.summary: Co-founded by Molly Huyck and Amie Konwinski, Aequitas Invest has launched a funding portal designed to address the systemic exclusion of women-led businesses from traditional venture capital. The platform aims to help female entrepreneurs raise capital while maintaining greater equity ownership.

startups-vc.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people's homes? Hello Robot is.
startups-vc.summary: Hello Robot has released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch, positioning the California startup at the forefront of residential robotics adoption. The product advancement signals growing market readiness for autonomous robots in consumer households.

startups-vc.title: Boston Startup Fundraising Looks Strong Only By Pre-AI Parameters
startups-vc.summary: Boston-area startups have attracted approximately $7.8 billion in funding year-to-date, positioning the region for its strongest annual tally in four years. However, the growth appears modest when benchmarked against the explosive capital flows to AI-focused companies, highlighting a broader geographic concentration of AI investment.

startups-vc.title: A startup, Everand, is now bundling e-books, audiobooks, and book clubs in challenge to Amazon
startups-vc.summary: Everand has launched a comprehensive reading subscription service that bundles e-books, audiobooks, and community book clubs, directly challenging Amazon's dominance in digital reading. The platform represents a niche alternative emphasizing community engagement in the broader media consumption market.

startups-vc.title: Active Startup Investors Didn't Hold Back In May
startups-vc.summary: May's active investor data reveals that the startup funding landscape remains dominated by established venture firms, demonstrating the continued concentration of capital among elite investors. The market dynamics suggest incumbent players maintain significant influence over allocation decisions in the current funding environment.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto Clarity Act in spotlight for bad-actor provisions as Senate process grinds forward
crypto-web3.summary: The cryptocurrency industry is actively promoting the Clarity Act to lawmakers, emphasizing its provisions that would provide law enforcement with enhanced tools to combat illicit finance and cryptocurrency-related criminal activity. The legislation continues to advance through the Senate amid ongoing debate over its regulatory framework and enforcement mechanisms.

crypto-web3.title: White House crypto adviser Witt defends Clarity Act, calls it 'pro-law enforcement' as lawmakers race to pass bill
crypto-web3.summary: Patrick Witt, the White House's top cryptocurrency adviser, defended the Clarity Act as a pro-enforcement measure despite criticism from certain quarters. The administration's backing underscores the bill's momentum in Congress as lawmakers work to pass legislation this session.

crypto-web3.title: JPMorgan says crypto bill may have only a narrow window for passage this year
crypto-web3.summary: JPMorgan analysts have warned that the Clarity Act may face a limited timeframe for passage in the current legislative session. The assessment suggests that regulatory and political headwinds could constrain opportunities to advance comprehensive crypto market structure legislation.

crypto-web3.title: 'The rally that wasn't': Bitcoin slides 14% in one week as ETF outflows, Strategy sale and oil prices hit sentiment
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin declined approximately 14 percent over one week, falling near $62,000 amid significant headwinds including $4.2 billion in exchange-traded fund outflows, a major cryptocurrency holder's liquidation, and broader macroeconomic pressures. The selloff reflects weakening market sentiment driven by multiple concurrent negative catalysts.

crypto-web3.title: Immunefi says DeFi is 'getting safer' as exploit losses fall 74% from 2022 peak amid AI-driven security arms race
crypto-web3.summary: Decentralized finance exploit losses declined 74 percent from their 2022 peak to $680 million in 2025, according to security platform Immunefi, reflecting structural improvements and increased adoption of AI-powered security tools. The data indicates the DeFi sector's maturation and strengthening defensive mechanisms against vulnerabilities.

crypto-web3.title: Senate Republicans urged financial regulators to rework bank capital rules for digital assets
crypto-web3.summary: A group of Senate Republicans has pressed key financial regulatory agencies to clarify and modify capital standards applicable to digital assets held by traditional financial institutions. The effort aims to facilitate greater institutional participation in the crypto market by reducing regulatory compliance burdens.

crypto-web3.title: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
crypto-web3.summary: Industry analysts have drawn parallels between the emerging tokenization trend and the early development of exchange-traded funds, suggesting that tokenization could evolve into a market structure shift comparable to ETFs' transformation into a $10 trillion-plus asset class. The analysis highlights blockchain-based tokenization's potential to reshape financial markets infrastructure.

crypto-web3.title: Not all Ethereum layer 2s are dying, but many general-purpose chains no longer have a reason to exist
crypto-web3.summary: The Ethereum layer-2 ecosystem is undergoing significant consolidation, with many general-purpose scaling chains facing existential challenges as specialized alternatives gain traction. The development suggests a market maturation process in which only differentiated or superior layer-2 solutions will sustain long-term viability.

crypto-web3.title: Visa, Brale test privacy-enabled SBC stablecoin settlement on Canton Network
crypto-web3.summary: Visa and fintech firm Brale have initiated a proof-of-concept demonstrating private, regulated stablecoin settlement functionality on the Canton Network blockchain. The trial explores how enterprise-grade privacy controls can be integrated with stablecoin payments to meet institutional compliance requirements.

crypto-web3.title: Ether.fi allocates $100 million to a Plume RWA vault, giving users access to yield
crypto-web3.summary: Liquid staking platform ether.fi has committed $100 million in capital to a real-world asset vault on the Plume network, enabling users to access yield opportunities tied to tangible assets. The allocation combines liquidity provider funds and existing managed capital from ether.fi's ecosystem.

energy-climate.title: AI boom means US is now 'investing more' in fossil-fuel power than China
energy-climate.summary: The surge in data centre development driven by artificial intelligence expansion is accelerating natural gas investments in the United States, surpassing Chinese fossil fuel power investments. This trend underscores the tension between AI infrastructure growth and climate mitigation objectives.

energy-climate.title: EM-DAT: Trump aid cuts could close database storing 'world's memory of disasters'
energy-climate.summary: Proposed Trump administration aid cuts threaten the closure of EM-DAT, the world's most comprehensive disaster database relied upon by thousands of climate scientists and policymakers globally. The loss of this critical resource would significantly impair climate research and disaster risk assessment capabilities worldwide.

energy-climate.title: US adds 6.4 GW of utility-scale solar, wind, storage in Q1
energy-climate.summary: The United States added 6.4 gigawatts of utility-scale renewable energy capacity in the first quarter of 2026, bringing total clean power capacity to 370 GW, sufficient to power approximately 80 million homes. The pipeline for new renewable projects grew 6 percent year-over-year, demonstrating sustained momentum in clean energy deployment.

energy-climate.title: European Commission to conduct risk assessment of EU solar, wind installations
energy-climate.summary: The European Commission announced a strategic roadmap for digitalization and artificial intelligence in energy that includes comprehensive risk assessments of solar and wind installations across the EU to address cybersecurity vulnerabilities. The initiative also aims to review and strengthen the energy security framework across member states.

energy-climate.title: Cybersecurity concerns put focus on India's solar inverter supplies
energy-climate.summary: Growing cybersecurity concerns regarding solar inverter supplies from high-risk vendors, particularly China, are prompting regulatory attention in India, though experts advocate for a balanced approach to strengthen security oversight without disrupting solar deployment. The EU has already considered funding restrictions for PV projects using inverters from flagged suppliers.

energy-climate.title: UK hits 22.3 GW solar capacity as cost per kW falls
energy-climate.summary: The United Kingdom's installed solar capacity reached 22.3 gigawatts across 2.026 million installations as of April 2026, representing 11.2 percent growth over the previous 12 months while per-kilowatt costs continue to decline. This milestone reflects accelerating adoption of distributed solar energy across residential, commercial, and utility-scale segments.

energy-climate.title: Malaysia forecast to reach 29.7 GW of solar by 2035
energy-climate.summary: Malaysia is on track to achieve 29.7 gigawatts of installed solar capacity by 2035, positioning the nation to exceed its 2040 renewable energy targets by approximately nine years. This accelerated timeline reflects Malaysia's strengthening commitment to renewable energy deployment and energy transition objectives.

energy-climate.title: Qualitas Energy funds 211MW battery projects in Italy
energy-climate.summary: Qualitas Energy has secured financing for a 211-megawatt battery energy storage system portfolio in Italy, bolstering grid stability and renewable energy integration capabilities across the country. This investment supports Italy's renewable energy expansion and energy transition infrastructure development.

energy-climate.title: Colorado co-op delivers 100% renewables in March, a first
energy-climate.summary: Holy Cross Energy, a Colorado electric cooperative, achieved 100 percent renewable energy generation in March 2026, marking a significant milestone for the utility. The cooperative plans to expand smart electrification programs and demand flexibility initiatives while selectively adding flexible renewable resources.

energy-climate.title: Mauritius tenders 220 MW of solar and storage via new 'e-Marketplace'
energy-climate.summary: Mauritius has launched tenders for 220 megawatts of combined solar and storage projects through a new e-Marketplace platform established by the International Solar Alliance to improve project bankability and financing access. The initiative specifically targets renewable energy investment in small island developing states facing unique energy transition challenges.

asia-pacific.title: The real story of 11 Skies is not about a failed shopping centre
asia-pacific.summary:
Few things age faster than a business plan built for a world that no longer exists. That may be the most important lesson from the saga of 11 Skies, the vast retail, entertainment and commercial complex beside Hong Kong International Airport that now appears destined for a dramatic reset. Reports that the Airport Authority could take majority control of the project from its private developer have triggered debate over who should bear the cost and who made the wrong bets.
The real story of 11...

asia-pacific.title: South Koreans sweat over US$779,000 air conditioning plan for prisons
asia-pacific.summary:
South Korea’s Ministry of Justice on Tuesday sought to defuse criticism over a 1.2 billion won (US$779,162) plan to install air conditioning in prisons, clarifying the equipment will cool corridors, not inmates’ cells.
The ministry framed the installation as a minimal measure to protect vulnerable inmates and correctional officers from extreme heat, while online critics condemned the taxpayer-funded project as an unfair convenience for criminals.
The ministry said the equipment will be installed

asia-pacific.title: China proposes nuclear-powered floating island to reshape global shipping
asia-pacific.summary:
A Chinese shipbuilder has unveiled a blueprint for a massive, nuclear-powered floating island that will serve as a container transfer terminal and a charging station for vessels.
The floating terminal will be powered by advanced molten salt reactors that use liquefied salt as both a fuel and coolant. These reactors can store vast amounts of thermal energy and cool without the need for water.
Jiangnan Shipyard, a subsidiary of state-owned China State Shipbuilding Corporation, said the complex...

asia-pacific.title: China pancake seller in US plans to run for mayor, generating buzz despite thorny past
asia-pacific.summary:
A Chinese pancake seller in the US has announced plans to run for mayor within three years, despite past controversy over an arrest.
Tang, 31, was born to a rural family in Sichuan, southwestern China, and left school after secondary education. He is the son of a security guard and a cleaner.
After working in sales and the car industry, he moved to the US in 2022, with little English, first taking a job at a used-car showroom.
Two years later, he settled in Los Angeles and began selling savoury.

asia-pacific.title: With fewer public spaces for Hong Kong’s helpers, where can they go to unwind?
asia-pacific.summary:
On a recent stormy Sunday, domestic helpers on their weekly day off were seen hastily erecting tents and laying out plastic sheets under footbridges and inside transport hubs in Hong Kong’s Tsuen Wan district as they sought refuge from the downpour.
Dozens of tents were pitched around the pillars of the footbridges, while other groups crowded onto narrow concrete platforms inside a bus terminus. Many leaned against metal railings or sat on the ground, laying down blankets and carving out spaces.

asia-pacific.title: Fatal crocodile attacks in Indonesia put spotlight on habitat destruction
asia-pacific.summary:
The recent deaths of two men from crocodile attacks in North Sumatra have put a spotlight on Indonesia’s record as the country with the world’s highest number of such killings annually, raising questions over whether habitat destruction is pushing people and wildlife into increasingly dangerous contact.
Environmental groups said the deaths reflected a wider pattern across the archipelago, where logging, plantations and other land-use changes had destabilised ecosystems.
The first incident was on

asia-pacific.title: US citizen and journalist charged with acting as Chinese agent
asia-pacific.summary:
A US citizen who lived and worked for state media in China for several years pleaded guilty on Thursday to acting as a foreign agent for Beijing after allegedly collecting US$100,000 in return for collecting intelligence in the US on “American targets” and American politicians.
According to court documents, Thomas Weir Pauken II worked at the “direction and control” of Chinese officials linked to China’s Ministry of State Security from at least 2019 until February 2026, with someone using the...

asia-pacific.title: Trump says he won’t nominate Pulte as permanent US spy chief after backlash
asia-pacific.summary:
US President ⁠Donald Trump on Thursday indicated ⁠that he would not nominate mortgage regulator ⁠Bill Pulte to be the nation’s intelligence chief once his temporary appointment expires early next year.
His comments follow a backlash against Pulte’s appointment as acting director of national intelligence from Democrats and some key senators of his Republican Party over the ‌Trump loyalist’s lack of national security experience.
Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, Trump indicated he would...

asia-pacific.title: Trump unveils plan to add ‘Trump promenade’ to Lincoln Memorial
asia-pacific.summary:
President Donald Trump on Thursday announced plans to add a potential “Trump promenade” to the iconic Lincoln Memorial, in his latest grandiose bid to leave his mark on the US capital.
Trump said the walkway would link the huge marble monument, built to commemorate Civil War-era president Abraham Lincoln, to the nearby Potomac River.
“They want to call it the Trump Promenade,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office as he unveiled the project for the first time. “I don’t know if I want to do...

asia-pacific.title: Chinese satellite company releases images of Nvidia, Apple HQs
asia-pacific.summary:
After it was sanctioned by the US government for allegedly helping Iran, a Chinese satellite company has released high-definition images of the Nvidia and Apple headquarters in California’s Silicon Valley.
They were among photos posted on social media on Monday by Changguang Satellite based in Changchun, in northeast China.
The satellite images – captured by the company’s Jilin-1 constellation – offer a detailed bird’s-eye view of the region.
They include the futuristic Endeavour and Voyager...
